comment.body: Congratulations on getting blocked by Dimitriev x)) My impression is that he seems to be triggered by something... He is speculated to be the successor if something happens to Putin, but he doesn't have power over the military guys like Shoigu... Putin and the military can hardly afford stopping the war, and Dimitriev is either played by them, or is collaborating with them to hook the US to buy time (I suspect the former, but the US will get more or less the same result, that is, being played by Putin).

comment.body:
Still waking up but I looked at it a bit last night (with the annotated forecast from https://open.substack.com/chat/posts/2aab643b-d095-4c00-8b42-4f35379fb002
 as reference) ... right now ES puts SPX about 6830, which is still in the (very wide) negative speed territory, and is trending slightly downwards.

Given this is FOMC day, 6850 is a likely upper limit since at least implied volatility will spike going into it.  Conversely, participants do not expect the market to go below 6810-20, but there is not much conviction in that.

On the downside, MM's have a large number of calls around 6690 with high color, which will provide an increasingly solid wall throughout the day.

After noon if the market is above 6780, additional support will appear - but below 6770, it will become increasingly difficult to break above it, with that resistance band dropping to 6750 near EOD.
